title: To Pray Is to Wander About the Meaning of Life
subtitle: A record of light. A record of the strangeness of being alive.

prelude.opening: There are moments when the world seems to withdraw from its own noise, and something quietly begins to appear. Nothing extraordinary has happened. The streets remain the same, light still falls across familiar walls, children continue to play, seasons return as they always have. And yet, the ordinary begins to resist its own familiarity.
body: What follows is an ongoing body of work unfolding through photography, writing, and film. These are not separate practices or independent projects, but different ways of approaching the same experience. Each movement, each photographic story, and each fragment belongs to a longer wandering through the ordinary mysteries of being alive.

This work does not move toward conclusions. It lingers with places, people, gestures, and passing moments, allowing them to reveal themselves without asking them to become symbols or explanations. Some parts speak through images, others through words. Neither is complete on its own. Together, they move toward what neither can fully hold.
The pages that follow are not meant to be hurried through. They are invitations to look a little longer, to remain with what is already here before familiarity quietly covers it again.

end.line: Perhaps to pray is nothing more than this: to wander within the mystery of being alive without demanding that it resolve itself.
end.note: The manuscript is still unfolding. More will appear here in time.

notebook.intro: Writings that move alongside the work — the same inquiry, approached in language.

about: Habib Saleh is a Lebanese visual artist working across photography, film, and writing. Rather than approaching these as separate disciplines, he sees them as different ways of following the same inquiry, each capable of expressing what the others cannot.

Working primarily in Lebanon, Saleh photographs the people and places closest to him — family, villages, cities, and the quiet rhythms of everyday life. Guided by natural light, long-term observation, and a sensitivity to the ordinary, his work explores presence, memory, time, and the fragile coexistence of beauty and devastation. Rather than centering extraordinary events, his photographs remain attentive to what continues around them: the gestures, relationships, and atmospheres through which life quietly persists.

Writing moves alongside the images as an equal part of his practice, not as explanation but as another way of approaching what cannot be fully held by a photograph. Together, photography, film, and text form an ongoing body of work that reflects on perception, the limits of language, and the experience of being alive.
colophon.manuscriptNote: is the continuous body of work gathered here — a living manuscript that keeps unfolding. The printed book will one day emerge from it.
colophon.seam: Habib Saleh also photographs on commission.
